summary: Iteration 8 retest: the iter-7 critical bug on /api/portfolio?admin_all=true (KeyError -> 500) is FIXED — admin admin_all now returns 200 with user_id on every position row, and the legacy ownerless-doc migration ran on startup (9 watchlist + 205 predictions purged per request). The originally failing test test_admin_portfolio_admin_all_cross_user PASSES now. HOWEVER, the fix introduced a REGRESSION: data isolation on /api/portfolio is broken for non-admin users and for admin-scoped (admin_all not set) queries. 3 NEW tests now fail (test_portfolio_lots_isolated, test_non_admin_admin_all_silently_ignored_on_portfolio, test_admin_portfolio_without_admin_all_is_scoped). Root cause: server.py:836 uses `{**scope, 'user_id': {'$exists': True, '$ne': None}}` — the SECOND `user_id` key overwrites the scope's `user_id: <current_user_id>`, so the aggregation matches ALL position_lots regardless of owner. Confirmed with python repl. Net iter8 result: 33/36 PASS (was 34/36 in iter7). The class of failure changed from CRASH (500) to SILENT DATA LEAK — strictly worse.

critical.endpoint: GET /api/portfolio (no admin_all OR for non-admin user)
critical.issue: DATA ISOLATION BROKEN. server.py:836 `{'$match': {**scope, 'user_id': {'$exists': True, '$ne': None}}}` — the second 'user_id' key in the spread overwrites the per-user scope filter (`scope = {'user_id': current_user['id']}`). Result: any authenticated user gets ALL users' position_lots returned (then user_id is attached to each row in the response). Reproduction: user_a POST /api/portfolio/lots {ticker:AAPL,...}; user_b GET /api/portfolio -> response.positions contains AAPL with user_id=user_a.id. Same for admin GET /api/portfolio (without admin_all=true) which is supposed to be scoped to admin's own portfolio. Fix (one line): `match = dict(scope); match.setdefault('user_id', {'$exists': True, '$ne': None}); pipeline = [{'$match': match}, ...]` — i.e., only add the existence guard when scope did NOT already pin user_id (admin_all path).

minor.issue: Same anti-pattern risk: lots endpoint uses user_filter(... , {'ticker': ticker}) which is fine today, but if a future patch adds a user_id existence check via dict-spread it will repeat this bug. Consider centralising the 'add ownerless guard' into user_filter itself (only when scope has no user_id).

Still does not update password_hash when ADMIN_PASSWORD in .env changes (carry-over from iter7). Auth-playbook contract requires updating existing admin password on env change. Iter8 retest did not exercise this (admin password unchanged), but the code is unchanged.

Migration deletes ownerless docs at every startup. Purge already ran (per main agent: 9 watchlist + 205 predictions). Consider gating with a one-shot flag in a meta collection so restarts don't always re-scan every per-user collection.

action_items:
FIX (critical, one-line): server.py:835-838. Replace `{'$match': {**scope, 'user_id': {'$exists': True, '$ne': None}}}` with logic that ONLY adds the existence guard when scope does not already contain user_id. Suggested patch:
    match = dict(scope)
    match.setdefault('user_id', {'$exists': True, '$ne': None})
    pipeline = [{'$match': match}, {'$group': {...}}]
This preserves per-user scope AND still excludes ownerless docs in admin_all mode.
After fix, re-run pytest backend/tests/test_auth.py — all 36 tests should pass.
Audit the rest of the codebase for the same dict-spread-overwrite pattern (any `{**scope, 'user_id': ...}` or `{**user_filter(...), 'user_id': ...}`).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:836 — dict spread anti-pattern silently overwrites the scope. This converted a CRASH (iter7, 500) into a SILENT DATA LEAK (iter8, 200 with foreign rows) — a far more dangerous failure mode. Any future scope filter merge MUST use $and or explicit setdefault to be safe.
Repeat: server.py is now 1336 lines — splitting into modules (auth, portfolio, alerts, admin) would make these pipeline merges much harder to get wrong.

Test gap: test_auth.py::TestAdmin::test_admin_portfolio_admin_all_cross_user PASSED in iter8 only because admin_all path uses scope={} (no collision). The 'all 36 tests pass' narrative on the next fix MUST come from a clean run of EVERY isolation test, not just the originally-failing one — iter7 missed this because the bug only manifests when there exist cross-user lots AND a non-admin (or scoped admin) queries /portfolio.

success_rate.backend: 92% (33/36 auth tests; original 2 iter7-failures now pass, but 3 new regressions appeared)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@stocks.app / Admin#Stocks2026 (auto-seeded). Test users created on demand: TEST-{tag}-{uuid}@stocks.app / passw0rd!.
seed_data_creation: test_auth.py creates throwaway users (TEST-A-*, TEST-B-*, TEST-reg-*, TEST-bf-*, TEST-del-*); user_a is given an AAPL position_lot. Module-scope cleanup fixture deletes TEST- users via /api/admin/users/{id} which cascades watchlist/lots/targets/trades/alerts/predictions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter8 retest result: original iter7 bug (500 on admin_all=true) is fixed, but the same diff introduced a CRITICAL data-isolation regression on /api/portfolio caused by dict-spread overwrite at server.py:836. Three new failures (test_portfolio_lots_isolated, test_non_admin_admin_all_silently_ignored_on_portfolio, test_admin_portfolio_without_admin_all_is_scoped) share this one root cause. After main agent patches the $match builder, re-run only test_auth.py to verify all 36 pass. No other regressions observed; brute-force lockout, admin user-delete cascade, /me, /admin/users, alerts admin_all all still pass.
rca of the issue:
RCA: in server.py get_portfolio (lines 832-839), the new $match stage is built as `{**scope, 'user_id': {'$exists': True, '$ne': None}}`. Python dict-literal merge keeps the LAST value for duplicate keys, so when scope already contains `'user_id': '<current_user_id>'` (the normal case for non-admin users and for admin without admin_all=true), it gets silently replaced by the existence guard. The pipeline then matches ALL lots in the DB; the response wraps each one with `pos['user_id'] = uid`, leaking other users' positions to the caller. Reproduction: (1) login as user_a, POST /api/portfolio/lots {ticker:AAPL,qty:5,buy_price_usd:150,buy_date:'2024-01-01'}, (2) login as user_b, GET /api/portfolio -> 200 with positions including {ticker:'AAPL', user_id:'<user_a_id>',...}. Mitigation (one-line): build match dict and use setdefault so the existence guard only applies when scope didn't already pin user_id. Concretely:
    match = dict(scope)
    match.setdefault('user_id', {'$exists': True, '$ne': None})
    pipeline = [{'$match': match}, {'$group': {'_id': {'user_id': '$user_id', 'ticker': '$ticker'}}}]
Verification: confirmed via python REPL that `{**{'user_id': 'X'}, 'user_id': {'$exists': True}}` evaluates to `{'user_id': {'$exists': True}}` (scope lost).
